{{Quote|I was once a Jedi Knight. My name was Aidan Bok. I was in charge of guarding the old Jedi library, but I failed in my task.|The ghost of Aidan Bok to Tash Arranda|<ref name="GOF" />}}
'''Aidan Bok''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. At some point during his service to the Order, Bok was tasked with guarding the [[Jedi library]] on the [[Nespis VIII|ancient space station]] of [[Nespis VIII]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|My body is gone, and I still bear the wounds Vader gave me. I don't deserve to return to rest in the Force.|The ghost of Aidan Bok to Tash Arranda|<ref name="GOF" />}}
Born during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Aidan Bok was identified as [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and turned over to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of the Jedi. Eventually [[Initiate Trials/Legends|graduating]] and being selected as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] by a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], Bok traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] until he was ready to pass the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]]. Once a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], Bok accepted solo missions from the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]].<ref name="GOF" />

As the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted across the Republic, the High Council assigned Bok to safeguard the Order's ancient storehouses on the [[Nespis VIII|Space City]] orbiting [[Nespis VIII]]. Remaining on the station for years, Bok was not killed during the execution of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] by the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] as he was not directly involved with the war. In isolation amongst the ancient relics, Bok was eventually tracked down by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] called [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Engaging the Sith in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]], Bok was slain by Vader and the storehouse fell to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="GOF" />

Years after his death, during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a young girl named [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash Arranda]] infiltrated the station in search of information on the Jedi. Discovering and reading ''[[The History of the Jedi Knights]]'', Arranda was visited by the [[Force ghost/Legends|lingering spirit]] of Bok. Unable to come to terms with his failure to the Order, Bok had been stuck at the station after his death. When Arranda was discovered by Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Borborygmus Gog]], Bok helped the girl escape and was finally able to become one with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="GOF" />
